A last consequence of  Theorem 4.8  is the following:

Corollary 4.11 
                            
                               A nontrivial strongly comonotonic bi-preference with a connected order-section topology is a transitive  NaP -preference.

A nontrivial strongly comonotonic bi-preference with a connected order-section topology is a transitive  NaP -preference.

Proof 
                            If  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     R 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is a strongly comonotonic bi-preference on  
                                  X 
                                such that  
                                  
                                     
                                        τ 
                                     
                                     
                                        R 
                                        , 
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        ↑ 
                                        ↓ 
                                     
                                  
                                is connected, then  
                                  S 
                                is transitive and complete by  Theorem 4.8 . Thus the claim readily follows from  Lemma 2.15 . ■

If  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     R 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is a strongly comonotonic bi-preference on  
                                  X 
                                such that  
                                  
                                     
                                        τ 
                                     
                                     
                                        R 
                                        , 
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        ↑ 
                                        ↓ 
                                     
                                  
                                is connected, then  
                                  S 
                                is transitive and complete by  Theorem 4.8 . Thus the claim readily follows from  Lemma 2.15 . ■

We conclude this section by exhibiting an intransitive  NaP -preference with a connected order-section topology.

Example 4.12 
                            Scott-Suppes Representable Semiorder,  Scott & Suppes, 1958 
                            
                            Let  
                                  S 
                                be the binary relation on  
                                  R 
                                defined as follows for each  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               :  
                                  
                                     
                                        
                                           x 
                                           
                                           S 
                                           
                                           y 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 ⟺ 
                                              
                                              
                                                 
                                                    def 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           x 
                                           + 
                                           1 
                                           ≥ 
                                           y 
                                           . 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               Notice that  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                if and only if  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     > 
                                     y 
                                     + 
                                     1 
                                  
                               , hence  
                                  S 
                                is quasi-transitive; further,  
                                  S 
                                is not transitive because of  
                                  
                                     
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        ∼ 
                                     
                                  
                               . In fact,  
                                  S 
                                is a  universal  Scott-Suppes representable semiorder, 
                                  33 
                                
                               
                                  33 
                                  After  Scott and Suppes (1958) , a semiorder  
                                        S 
                                      on  
                                        X 
                                      is  Scott-Suppes representable  if there is a real-valued function  
                                        
                                           u 
                                           : 
                                           X 
                                           → 
                                           R

                                           y

                                           1 
                                           ≥ 
                                           u

                                              y 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                        
                                     . 
                                in the sense that any other Scott-Suppes representable semiorder embeds into it. The trace  
                                  
                                     
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        tr 
                                     
                                  
                                of  
                                  S 
                                is the usual linear order  
                                  ≥ 
                                of the reals.

                                     R 
                                     × 
                                     R 
                                  
                               . It follows that the tracing bi-preference  
                                  
                                     (

                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is a nontrivial  NaP -preference with the property that

Our second main result is about ‘everywhere nontrivial’ comonotonic bi-preferences. Everywhere nontriviality accounts for a mild richness of the strict soft component:

Definition 5.1 
                         A weak preference  
                               S

                               X

                                  R 
                                  , 
                                  S 
                                  )

                               S 
                             is everywhere nontrivial.

                               S

The property described in  Definition 5.1  is satisfied in most economic settings: for instance, it is trivially implied by the property of  local nonsatiation  in classical demand theory.

We shall prove the following counterpart of  Theorem 4.8 , where ‘everywhere nontrivial’ replaces ‘weakly monotonic’ (but in this case the  NaP -preference may fail to be transitive):

For nontrivial bi-preferences, the two properties of (i) strong comonotonicity and (ii) everywhere nontrivial comonotonicity are, in general, independent of each other. For instance, the nontrivial bi-preference

                            R

                            S

The remainder of this section is devoted to a formal proof of  Theorem 5.2 .

Recall from Section  2.1  that a strong quasi-preorder is a  strongly quasi-transitive  preference  
                            S 
                         , i.e., the inclusions  
                            
                               S 
                               ∘ 
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     > 
                                  
                               
                               ⊆ 
                               S 
                            
                          and  
                            
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                  
                                  
                                     > 
                                  
                               
                               ∘ 
                               S 
                               ⊆ 
                               S 
                            
                          hold. Strong quasi-transitivity implies quasi-transitivity, and the converse holds under completeness. 
                            36 
                          
                         
                            36 
                            See Footnote 15.

The key result to derive  Theorem 5.2  is the  Decomposition Lemma  ( Lemma 5.10 ), which fully describes the structure of a strong quasi-preorder that is also splitting. To state this result, we need some new notions that concern a single binary relation.

Remark 5.5 
                            In graph-theoretic terms, the pair  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                can be looked at as a directed graph, where  
                                  
                                     e 
                                     = 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        a 
                                        , 
                                        b 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                is an edge in  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                if and only if  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . Then two sets  
                                  A 
                                and  
                                  B 
                                are incomparable (with respect to  
                                  S 
                               ) if there is no directed edge between an element of  
                                  A 
                                and element of  
                                  B 
                               . Notice that being incomparable sets says nothing about the internal structure of each of the two sets: in fact, there may well be directed edges inside  
                                  A 
                                and  
                                  B 
                               . A similar remark applies for the notion of indifferent sets.

                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 < 
                                              
                                           
                                           b 
                                        
                                     , (2)  
                                        
                                           a 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 < 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 < 
                                              
                                           
                                           b 
                                        
                                     , (3)  
                                        
                                           a 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 3 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                           b 
                                        
                                     , etc. 
                                Define a binary relation  
                                  ≈ 
                                on  
                                  X 
                                by letting, for each  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     , 
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               ,  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ≈ 
                                     b 
                                  
                                if there is a mixed strict path from  
                                  a 
                                to  
                                  b 
                               . Then  
                                  ≈ 
                                is an equivalence relation on  
                                  X 
                               : we shall denote by  
                                  
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        a 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                the equivalence class of  
                                  a 
                               .

Remark 5.7 
                            In graph-theoretic terms, if  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is the  undirected  graph obtained from the directed graph  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                by transforming each directed edge into an undirected edge, then the equivalence classes of  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                with respect to  
                                  ≈ 
                                are exactly the  connected components  of  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , that is, the maximal connected subsets of the graph  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               .

In graph-theoretic terms, if  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is the  undirected  graph obtained from the directed graph  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                by transforming each directed edge into an undirected edge, then the equivalence classes of  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                with respect to  
                                  ≈ 
                                are exactly the  connected components  of  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               , that is, the maximal connected subsets of the graph  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     X 
                                     , 
                                     T 
                                     ) 
                                  
                               .

Strong quasi-preorders that are also splitting satisfy an additional property:

Lemma 5.9 
                            
                               Assume  
                               
                                  S 
                                
                               is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting. For all  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               
                               , if  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               then  
                               
                                  
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               .

Assume  
                               
                                  S 
                                
                               is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting. For all  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     , 
                                     y 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               
                               , if  
                               
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                
                               then  
                               
                                  
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               .

Proof 
                            Assume  
                                  S 
                                is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting. 
                            
                               Claim:  
                               For  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     , 
                                     B 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               
                               , if  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     B 
                                  
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ∈ 
                                     A 
                                  
                               
                               , then  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           A 
                                           ∪ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ↓ 
                                                 , 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       S 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       > 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∪ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ↑ 
                                                 , 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       S 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       > 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     B 
                                  
                               
                               . 
                            
                            To prove the Claim, let  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     ∪ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↑ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     B 
                                  
                               . We show that  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . Without loss of generality, suppose  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               , i.e.,  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     z 
                                  
                               . If  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               , then  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                                by the strong quasi-transitivity of  
                                  S 
                               , which contradicts  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . Further, we cannot have  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     z 
                                  
                               , since otherwise  
                                  z 
                                would contradict the fact that  
                                  S 
                                is splitting. It follows that  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . This proves the Claim. 
                            Now apply iteratively the Claim to all  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               , and get  
                                  
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               . This completes the proof. ■

Assume  
                                  S 
                                is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting.

Claim:  
                               For  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     , 
                                     B 
                                     ⊆ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               
                               , if  
                               
                                  
                                     A 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     B 
                                  
                                
                               and  
                               
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ∈ 
                                     A 
                                  
                               
                               , then  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        
                                           A 
                                           ∪ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ↓ 
                                                 , 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       S 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       > 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                           ∪ 
                                           
                                              
                                                 a 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ↑ 
                                                 , 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       S 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       > 
                                                    
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     B 
                                  
                               
                               .

To prove the Claim, let  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     ∪ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↑ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     B 
                                  
                               . We show that  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . Without loss of generality, suppose  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     
                                        
                                           a 
                                        
                                        
                                           ↓ 
                                           , 
                                           
                                              
                                                 S 
                                              
                                              
                                                 > 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                  
                               , i.e.,  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     z 
                                  
                               . If  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               , then  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                                by the strong quasi-transitivity of  
                                  S 
                               , which contradicts  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . Further, we cannot have  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     z 
                                  
                               , since otherwise  
                                  z 
                                would contradict the fact that  
                                  S 
                                is splitting. It follows that  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     b 
                                  
                               . This proves the Claim.

Now apply iteratively the Claim to all  
                                  
                                     a 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                                and  
                                  
                                     b 
                                     ∈ 
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               , and get  
                                  
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     Mix 
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        y 
                                        ) 
                                     
                                  
                               . This completes the proof. ■

We can finally state the key result to prove  Theorem 5.2 .

Lemma 5.10 
                            Decomposition Lemma 
                            
                               If  
                               
                                  S 
                                
                               is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting, then  
                               
                                  X 
                                
                               is the disjoint union of sets  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               such that each restriction  
                               
                                  
                                     S 
                                     ↾ 
                                     
                                        
                                           X 
                                        
                                        
                                           i 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is complete, and distinct  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                     
                                        i 
                                     
                                  
                               
                               ’s are either indifferent sets or incomparable sets.

If  
                               
                                  S 
                                
                               is strongly quasi-transitive and splitting, then  
                               
                                  X

                                  ≈

Notice also that the definition of mixed strict path implies that we can never have a relation of strict preference between elements of distinct equivalence classes. Finally,  Lemma 5.8  rules out the possibility that there are distinct

The next two results link properties of bi-preferences to properties of single preferences.

Lemma 5.11 
                            
                               The soft component of a comonotonic bi-preference is strongly quasi-transitive.

The soft component of a comonotonic bi-preference is strongly quasi-transitive.

Proof

                                     ( 
                                     R 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                be a comonotonic bi-preference on  
                                  X 
                               . By symmetry, it suffices to show that

                                     ∘

                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     ⊆ 
                                     S

                                     x

                                     y

                                     X

                                     x 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                for some  
                                  
                                     z 
                                     ∈ 
                                     X 
                                  
                               . Now Comonotonicity yields  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     z 
                                     
                                     
                                        
                                           R 
                                        
                                        
                                           > 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                               , and Transitive Coherence entails  
                                  
                                     x 
                                     
                                     S 
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                               , as required.  ■

Lemma 5.12 
                            
                               The following statements are equivalent for a bi-preference  
                               
                                  
                                     (

                                     S

                               such that

                                        ↓

                                              ( 
                                              R 
                                              , 
                                              S 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                         
                                        is weakly comonotonic; 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     (ii) 
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                         
                                        is splitting.

The following statements are equivalent for a bi-preference  
                               
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     R

                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                
                               such that  
                               
                                  
                                     
                                        τ 
                                     
                                     
                                        R 
                                        , 
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        ↑ 
                                        ↓ 
                                     
                                  
                                
                               is connected and  
                               
                                  S

                                              ( 
                                              R 
                                              , 
                                              S 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                         
                                        is weakly comonotonic; 
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     (ii) 
                                     
                                        
                                           S 
                                         
                                        is splitting.

( 
                                              R 
                                              , 
                                              S 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                         
                                        is weakly comonotonic;

S 
                                         
                                        is splitting.

Proof 
                            Assume that  
                                  R 
                                and  
                                  
                                     
                                        S 
                                     
                                     
                                        > 
                                     
                                  
                                are semicontinuous, and  
                                  S 
                                is strongly quasi-transitive. If  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     R 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is weakly comonotonic, then  
                                  S 
                                is splitting by  Lemma 4.7 : thus (i) implies (ii). Conversely, if  
                                  S 
                                is splitting, the Decomposition Lemma ensures that both  
                                  
                                     
                                        ( 
                                        x 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              S 
                                           
                                           
                                              > 
                                           
                                        
                                        z 
                                        ∧ 
                                        y

                                        x

                                              S 
                                           
                                           
                                              > 
                                           
                                        
                                        z

                                           S 
                                        
                                        
                                           ⊥ 
                                        
                                     
                                     y 
                                  
                                never happen, hence  
                                  
                                     ( 
                                     R 
                                     , 
                                     S 
                                     ) 
                                  
                                is (vacuously) weakly comonotonic.  ■

                                  R 
                                and
